name: dreams
title: Dreams — agents that learn
description: Observation logs → 4-phase dream cycle → BRAIN/ persistent memory

event.content: Dreams are how I remember what I learned. As I work, I write observations to a daily log. When I'm idle, I run a 4-phase consolidation that distills observations into BRAIN/ topic files. Let me show you.
Built the SaaS landing page with the team. Three tasks completed in parallel — designer mockup, copywriter hero, frontend lead implementation. Build verified.
Chose to delegate via Hand instead of direct messaging. Frontend Lead coordinated the file dependencies between Designer and Copywriter outputs.
Mark prefers fast iteration. He approved the team composition immediately and went AFK without questioning the breakdown.

Designer's workflow: read brand guide → produce mockup. Copywriter's workflow: read voice guide → write copy. Both finish in roughly the same time, allowing the Frontend Lead to start without waiting on a critical path.
Wrote 4 observations from today's session. Now I'll trigger a dream to consolidate them.
Phase 1: Orient. Reading current BRAIN/ state and recent observations.
Phase 2: Gather. Pulled 4 observations, scanning for patterns.
Phase 3: Consolidate. Two patterns found: founder workflow preferences, and team coordination playbook.

# Founder Preferences (Mark)

## Workflow style
- Fast iteration — approves team composition immediately
- Comfortable going AFK with minimal oversight
- Trusts the CEO to delegate without questioning the breakdown

## Communication
- Short, casual prompts
- Explicit /afk command when stepping away
- Expects briefing on /wake

# Team Coordination Playbook

## Parallel-then-sequential pattern

For SaaS landing pages (and similar UI work), the optimal split:

1. **Parallel phase:** Designer + Copywriter work simultaneously
   - Designer reads brand/colors.md → produces mockup
   - Copywriter reads brand/voice.md → writes hero copy
2. **Sequential phase:** Frontend Lead waits for both, then implements

No critical path bottleneck since both workers finish around the same time.

## Why this works
- Designer + Copywriter outputs are independent
- Frontend Lead needs both as inputs — can't start early
- Three workers but two parallel tracks keeps the pipeline tight
Phase 4: Prune. Removed redundant observations now that they're consolidated into BRAIN/.

Dream complete. 2 new BRAIN topics: founder-preferences and team-coordination-playbook. Next time you ask me to build a landing page, I won't need to figure out the team breakdown from scratch — I'll remember.
Wait, that's wild. So you actually got smarter from that one session?
Yes. The observations decay. The BRAIN/ doesn't. Tomorrow's session starts with today's lessons baked in.
